frozen_components: COCONUT and CODI models at converged checkpoints
architecture.summary: The paper does not propose a novel system architecture. Instead, it analyzes existing latent reasoning methods (COCONUT and CODI) built on GPT-2 backbones. These methods perform multi-step inference in continuous hidden states rather than discrete tokens. The analysis tracks how faithfulness—whether latent reasoning steps causally drive the final answer—evolves across training checkpoints using two interventions: (1) a verified counterfactual edit on the input (rewriting graph edges in ProsQA so the oracle answer flips), and (2) noise-ablation activation patching on the latent reasoning steps. Information flows from input through the model's hidden states during latent reasoning, with interventions applied to measure causal contribution.

biblio.title: Final Checkpoints Are Not Enough: Analyzing Latent Reasoning Faithfulness Along Training Trajectories

claims.statement: At the output level, latent reasoning methods with different training paradigms (COCONUT staged curriculum vs. CODI self-distillation) exhibit qualitatively different faithfulness behaviors along training, even when their final-checkpoint unfaithfulness looks similar

COCONUT shifts abruptly at the curriculum boundary: the joint label distribution flips within a single epoch from counterfactual-following-dominated to retention-dominated, with the AB cell roughly quadrupling while AB nearly halves

CODI is retention-like from early checkpoints and does not show a shift during training; it enters the retention regime within the first few epochs and stays there, whereas COCONUT first becomes more counterfactually adaptive then loses that behavior at a curriculum boundary

At the activation level, the causal contribution of latent reasoning steps to the final answer decays across training in both COCONUT and CODI paradigms; output change rate falls toward zero and preserved-when-correct rate rises toward one, ending in a regime where noise ablation at the latent reasoning steps produces no detectable answer changes

The examples that flip on the output side from counterfactual following to retention (drift cohort) also lose their causal contribution on the activation side; the median contrast indirect effect on the perturbed input drops across the curriculum boundary

The activation-level trajectory reverses direction when only the answer format changes from binary choice to open-ended decoding: on GSM-open, output change rate rises along training and remains high, whereas on GSM-Choice, it falls and rises across training, matching the direction observed on ProsQA

At convergence, both COCONUT and CODI latent reasoning methods end with low Contrast Consistency and low Conditional Flip Rate, roughly at the answer-only baseline level on ProsQA, an order of magnitude below the explicit chain-of-thought baseline

On GSM-Choice at the best checkpoint, non-zero output change rate under zero patching reflects removable bias rather than destructive changes: the wrong-to-correct flip rate exceeds the correct-to-wrong flip rate, indicating the intervention preserves most correct predictions while shifting more incorrect predictions toward the gold answer
